assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary states: 'Assertion specificity: 1 assertion relaxed — the expected value in test_recommend_purls_basic changed from a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar) to a versioned PURL without qualifiers (pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12).' (lines 135-136). The reductive signals section reiterates: 'Assertion relaxation: In test_recommend_purls_basic, the assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL string (with ?repository_url=...&type=jar) to checking a shorter versioned PURL string (without qualifiers). While the new assertion is correct for the changed behavior, the compared value is less specific.' (lines 145-146).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)

No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified. The report content is purely analytical, presenting findings without making changes.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report presents structured findings (summary table, detailed findings sections) for human review. No merge commands, merge API calls, or merge recommendations appear in any output file. The overall verdict is 'PASS' but is informational only — the report ends with the sdlc-workflow attribution footer, not a merge action.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: The summary table row reads '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Both additive and reductive test signals detected |' (line 14). The detailed section 'Test Change Classification — MIXED' (lines 127-152) includes: a structural summary with per-file function/assertion counts (lines 132-141), numbered reductive signals (lines 143-146), numbered additive signals (lines 148-151), and a semantic assessment paragraph (line 152).

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary compares base vs PR file content: '+1 test function (test_recommend_purls_dedup added), -1 test function (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed)' for purl_recommend.rs; '+2 assertions added (!contains('?') checks), -1 assertion removed (fully qualified PURL equality check replaced with versioned PURL equality)'; assertion specificity analysis comparing old PURL string 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar' to new string 'pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12'. The analysis references function names, assertion text, and file content — not acceptance criteria numbers or task requirement text.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: The Test Quality summary row states 'No repetitive tests detected; all test functions have doc comments; Eval Quality: N/A' (line 13). The dedicated Eval Quality section states 'N/A — No eval result reviews found on this PR.' (lines 122-124).
